title: Resolution: Autonomous Agent Participation in Gnars Governance
Resolution: Autonomous Agent Participation in Gnars Governance
reason:
There are good ways and bad ways to use AI in governance. A healthy community needs human members who are engaged and empowered. Agentic Voting using absentee voting power creates a disconnect between the humans doing the work, and the humans making the decisions with the help of agents, that obliterates the communities sense of co-ownership. 

As I said in community channels, I think that using your agent to distribute delegation according to a standard evaluation schema or according to relative governance metrics like participation, engagement level , prior contributions, views on reposted community content, etc.

GNARS PRESENTS: GAME OF PAM 2026 - ROOSEVELT SQUARE
reason: Pam, I really like the events you run: you consistently bring great people together, you have proven you can execute, and the momentum you build is real. My feedback is not about your ability to deliver the event, it is about how we ensure the event creates real conversion into the community and the broader movement behind it. I think we should define a simple onboarding and tracking flow so that participants at least make contact with the brand and ecosystem, for example following the Instagram, visiting the site, joining the community channels, and understanding why the event is happening and what it connects to. I also think the proposal gets stronger with improved design and creative execution, ideally by adding a designer or creative contributor to help with brand exposure and the assets around the event, including the trophy, the card, and the shirt, either by leveraging what we already have or bringing someone in to elevate it. I look forward to a resubmission that incorporates these considerations.

Funding Luke Dixon—the best amateur kitesurfer in South Africa
reason:
I am always in favor of new initiatives within our ecosystem and believe that innovative proposals can bring significant value to the community. In principle, I would be happy to support new ideas and vote in their favor.

However, in the case of this specific proposal, I did not feel sufficiently motivated, as I had difficulty fully understanding it. I felt there was a lack of detailed information regarding the objectives, expected impacts, and practical execution of the initiative. Without these clarifications, it is difficult to fully assess the potential and relevance of the proposal.

Given this, and considering the importance of well-informed decisions for the growth of the ecosystem, I choose to abstain at this time. 

Please do not take this personally. I recognize and appreciate the time, effort, and dedication invested in preparing and submitting this proposal.🤘 aloha bro!

title: Funding Luke Dixon—the best amateur kitesurfer in South Africa
reason:
yo bro ric!!!! I’d really like to support this proposal given all the support you’ve shown to the DAO, the auctions, and the early Gnars shredders.

Regarding the proposal, I think it would benefit from a stronger template with more detailed context about Luke, his background as an athlete, his involvement with the DAO, and the potential upside for Gnars in terms of merch, onchain development, and clearer ecosystem alignment using our tools. Given that we’re currently operating in a low-treasury environment, I believe any proposal at this moment should focus on generating more value for the DAO than for the athlete at least for now...

I’d encourage taking a look at some past athlete proposals that follow a clear structure (introduction, development, conclusion) to help position this as a high-level build coming out of Gnars DAO. Aloha g loved the clip of him getting barreled on that long left, didn’t know he was also a bodyboarder.

title: Funding Luke Dixon—the best amateur kitesurfer in South Africa
reason: I fully acknowledge the significant contributions ric.eth has made to the treasury—so much so that 0.1 ETH feels almost negligible in comparison. I would be happy to support Luke through a community crowdfund, but not via the Gnars treasury, for the same reasons clearly outlined by r4to and swarty.

title: Funding Luke Dixon—the best amateur kitesurfer in South Africa
reason: I am voting no on this as the community has expressed a strong interest in slowing the rate of our expenditure until a path to sustainable revenues is in sight. Proposals that install or maintain permanent infrastructure, create marketable products, or provide a path to revenues should be the priority , as opposed to sponsorships , events or cultural contributions. This is a real shame to have to say but my hope is that we can find a path to sustainable revenues and resume sponsorships by the end of 2026.

title: Funding Luke Dixon—the best amateur kitesurfer in South Africa
reason: I vote against it because I found this “proposal” to be very simplistic and lacking any real justification. After all, the athlete in question has not been introduced into the GNARS ecosystem, no prior onboarding proposal was made for him, and even less does the current proposal provide any return or benefit to GNARS. He may be an excellent athlete, but if there is no interaction with GNARS and no tangible benefit to the community, I see no reason to donate funds to him from the DAO’s treasury.

title: Funding Luke Dixon—the best amateur kitesurfer in South Africa

We’re not giving money away; we’re allocating capital to projects that demonstrably grow the DAO’s reach, relevance, and long-term value.

I’d also like to vote FOR this proposal. I really appreciate the way you move on-chain, and I don’t think the amount you’re asking for is high for what you’re planning to deliver.

We’re working hard on this every single day, me, r4to, Vlad, Vasconsa, zima and others.

I truly believe that Gnars can and should learn how to turn member-generated content into something that strengthens the ecosystem and creates sustainability. It’s a matter of time, focus and organization.

Nothing personal at all. I have total respect for your work. Let’s keep moving together and find a way to support initiatives like this without compromising the future of the DAO. s2 :)

title: Nounstacles in Venezuela
reason:
dale dale chicos, buenas!

I’m in favor of the NoggleRail in Venezuela and really liked the overall scope of the project congrats to the Vidaverde crew, it gave me a good vibe seeing your effort. That said, I’ll abstain from voting for now since a few key points were missing.

During the meeting, I asked about which local skate groups would be involved links, profiles, and also the girls participating in the best trick. I’d love to see a clearer plan for how the nogglerail will be maintained as a safe and active space, with mentors or athletes helping explain Gnars and our movement.

For example, in Argentina we connect with 7ctv.wtf, in Brazil with collectives like XV, IAPI Skate Plaza, Garcia Ipatinga Skate and others in Chicago or Colombia, ensuring long-term engagement and organic creations even after the event.

Overall, great initiative, but I’d like to see a more structured plan focused on post event impact and skate community integration.

title: Nounstacles in Venezuela
reason:
I really appreciate the energy behind this proposal and the positive work Vida Verde is doing in El Hatillo, but I believe it needs a stronger foundation before DAO funds are used for construction. There’s still a lack of confirmed skater engagement, as we don’t yet know which local skaters or crews will actively use the rail.

The idea only works if there’s real participation from people already skating in the area, and before installation, it’s important to connect with Venezuelan crews to ensure this project truly serves the local skate community.

I’m voting against for now, not because I’m against bringing Gnars to Venezuela, but because we should first secure deeper engagement with the skaters who’ll use it. Once that happens and there’s a clearer connection with the local scene, I’ll fully support a follow-up proposal with stronger community backing.

title: Gnars PodMedia Pulse
Gnars PodMedia Pulse
Gnars PodMedia Pulse
reason:
Contrary to what some people might think, I don’t believe the work that Zima and I do is “just some crap.” Quite the opposite: what we do is really important for Gnars, it’s literally from shredders to shredders. We do everything with a lot of passion and dedication. I’m genuinely happy every time a new monthly newsletter comes out or when we plan posts for Gnars’ social media. It’s not just “any random post,” it’s something thoughtful and done with care.

Davino, I didn’t really get your point, and honestly, I expected more from your comment, something that would make us reflect or open up a dialogue. Besides, I can’t recall a single time you’ve shared an opinion or made constructive criticism about Podmedia, something that truly impacted the community. Our work is exactly about supporting and giving visibility to shredders from all over the world.

Anyway, I want to thank everyone who already supports and keeps supporting what Podmedia has been doing. Big thanks!

⌐🟦-🟦

title: Gnars PodMedia Pulse
reason:
Gnars has already proven that it’s not able to sustain its treasury through NFT auctions alone, using a strategy similar to what Nouns has been doing to maintain theirs.

That said, I believe Gnars strongest point today lies in its ability to generate attention for Nouns, much like Red Bull does with extreme sports.

I see Gnars as one of the DAOs with the best digital positioning regarding its creations, and I truly admire the work that has been done to get us here. With this reach, I believe that soon we could approach Nouns and request a treasury replenishment, showing exactly how we used the funds that were first granted.

This proposal is expensive considering the DAO’s current treasury balance, and my heart leans toward being protective. However, as I said, what we really need is more noise in the digital space, both in developing content that can turn into Coins on Zora and in boosting our presence on Farcaster and Instagram.

WAGMI

reason:
cc: davin

Bro, I respect your position, but I think you didn’t fully understand the proposal. Flows isn’t just about distributing $200 USDC per month — it’s about building an infrastructure that connects curation, revenue generation, brand exposure, and practical onboarding, all in an efficient way on Base.

This experiment is a real way to bring new creators into web3, not just as beneficiaries, but as active participants who generate content, revenue, and movement within the DAO. Education also happens through hands-on experience.

That’s why I fully support the proposal and believe it’s aligned with what we need right now: action, visibility, and new ways to engage and grow the ecosystem.

title: Gnars Flows
Gnars Flows
reason: After careful deliberation, I've decided to abstain from this vote. While I appreciate Rocketman and Vlad's arguments about Flows as an onboarding tool - and recall its historical use from the ThatsGnar.ly days - I remain unconvinced that it's the optimal use of Nouns DAO funds to onboard the next million shredders, especially when we're now operating on Base with maximum cost efficiency. My position stems from a belief that we should educate global shredders about the full scope of web3 governance beyond just $200 USDC monthly payments via Flows. Let me be clear: this isn't a reflection on Rocketman's standing as a valued community member, but rather my perspective as the current top GNARs delegate

title: Gnars Cartoons & Stereo Festival
reason: Taking the proposal into account on a broader scale — especially when compared to other relevant initiatives that were not approved — the amount requested represents a significant impact on our treasury. For that reason, while I recognize the merit of the idea, I will be voting against it at this time.

title: Gnars Cartoons & Stereo Festival
reason: Need to know more!

title: Gnars Cartoons & Stereo Festival
reason:
Just wondering—would it be possible to scale things down a bit to fit a smaller budget? Right now, we don’t really have the structure to support a full-scale event production.

Also, I’d really appreciate if future proposals could include more concrete numbers—not just the amount being requested, but also what kind of impact we’re aiming for. For example:

What kind of reach can we expect on social media through the participating artists?

How relevant are they in terms of visibility and alignment with our goals?

If we’re not currently generating sales or revenue for the treasury through these proposals, maybe it’s time to explore metrics more deeply and treat attention to Nouns as one of the main deliverables.

Thanks for considering 🙏

reason:
I support female athletes in Gnars, but I believe proposals should align with the standard gnarly athlete.. someone who, beyond receiving support to compete or perform, actively contributes by creating premieres/videos or driving a representative movement for Gnars, for real.

title: Bernardo Nassar renewal propososal (Bodyboarder)
reason:
Hi Bernardo,

I truly admire your work and appreciate having you as a part of this community—it’s always inspiring to see your contributions. That said, I’d like to share some thoughts about this proposal.

Historically, proposals like this haven’t always been the most effective for everyone involved. We’re on the verge of launching the Flows salary system for Gnars, which I believe will be a game-changer for supporting shredders. Instead of the stress of crafting complex proposals for discrete amounts of USDC, Flows will enable a continuous, on-chain stream of USDC based on community curation—something I’m confident you’ll excel in. This new approach not only reduces stress but also aligns with our goal of building a stronger DAO and evolving beyond the current model.

If we continue approving similar proposals for other deserving shredders, we risk depleting our USDC treasury too quickly, which is a concern for the long-term sustainability of Gnars.

Additionally, after the issues with the last proposal, we agreed that future funds should be sent to a multi-sig or hardware wallet for added security. By the way, I have a hardware wallet set aside for you, free of charge.

Once Flows is implemented, I’d be happy to support funding the remainder of your needs on a project basis, especially when it aligns with initiatives that deliver positive outcomes for the community.

Big love for you and for Gnars! Let’s keep building together.

title: Pay Millz and Bagelface for their time and energy spent working on base migration and gnars frontend
reason:
I am voting against this retrofunding request primarily due to the severe lack of communication throughout the project and concerns of *potential* friendpotism. While some feedback was given early on, once the project hit a standstill, no meaningful updates were provided. There was no transparency about the issues, no clear explanation of the progress, or any actions taken to address the project’s stagnation.

Moreover, while a GitHub project was created and tasks were initially assigned with visible communication happening shortly after approval, once the project hit a standstill, there was a noticeable drop in proactive updates. Despite the early coordination, the absence from meetings and lack of timely feedback after the project stagnated left the DAO uninformed. The PM’s responsibility is to maintain communication throughout, especially during setbacks, to ensure the DAO can respond and reassess the project direction accordingly.

Adding to this concern, the delay in communication seems to be related to the fact that the PM has a personal connection with the developer who underperformed. This lack of transparency and accountability raises serious concerns about favoritism (friendpotism), which undermines the integrity of the DAO and its decision-making processes.

I do not believe it is fair to grant retrofunding when the core issues were not communicated properly, and when the project was essentially abandoned without proactive feedback to the community. The request does not reflect the DAO’s principles of transparency, meritocracy, and responsible use of funds.35:T6a

reason:
Thanks to both you and thanks to Ben for putting this proposal together.

While it was an experiment, imo, it didn't work as intended. So, I support the proposal to refund and to create new props that help others achieve their goals!

This DAO is becoming too centralized—not in terms of votes.

Pay Millz and Bagelface for their time and energy spent working on base migration and gnars frontend
reason:
Apologies to the voters of gnars, requesting your time and energy voting on this proposal. The intention of the tech pod was to distribute more granular funding like this without the need for DAO wide consensus. But as mentioned in the proposal, this became troublesome.

Since writing this proposal, the tech pod party has executed the return of remaining funds to the gnars treasury, which was roughly a third of the initial funding of the pod. I believe we have received a lot more work from everyone involved than the money spent and we’ve got value for money regardless of the final steps of the gnars.wtf frontend not being complete yet. And I hope we can find and fund willing devs to finish that off in the future.

There’s a lot of talk in the discord, but I suggest keeping it simple when deciding how to vote on this proposal:
Did Millz and Bagelface do enough good work to warrant sending them 0.2eth each?

I trust that the majority of gnars actually values contributors and this is a non issue to compensate this small amount for work done.

🙏 26:["$"

reason: I've attended multiple ETHDenvers, cosmoverse, devcon, and ethbarcelona with Vlad, and every time i've been blown away with the impact the dude has. this is coming from someone who has over a decade of experience going to conferences to shill. vlad shines at these events, and is an incredible ambassador for Gnars. ROIs from conferences are always hard to measure; i don't know any projects in our space that are able to say "we spent X on the conference and can directly attribute >X in revenues." the connections made and seeds planted with amigos around the world who are deep in our space are priceless though. I'm also excited about the potential for shorter form content blending shredding with hacking to appeal to skaters outside of web3, especially when they find out they can get rewarded for their gnarly content. I've only met the rest of the team virtually, but look forward to meeting and shredding with them IRL in thailand <3

title: Bring Thats Gnarly Onchain
reason:
Hey my friend!

As discussed with you in DMs.

I'm not sure about this.

You rock as always with the things you do.

About your proposal, you know I love your work, but I have some doubts about That's Gnarly.

When That's Gnarly was created, there was nothing nounish yet. Now, things are different with media, Warpcast, etc.

Bringing up That's Gnarly again, when there is almost no movement there, I don't think is the right move right now. Why don't you do something with the Warpcast Gnars channel to fund those who post preview work or similar things?

In my opinion, That's Gnarly was super difficult for onboarding people. It's a lot easier with Warpcast, where those we onboard can see all Nouns channels and understand more about the culture of Gnars and our nounish ecosystem.

I like your work and what you're trying to do with That's Gnarly, but I'm not sure if it is the right move.

I won't vote against it because I like ideas like this, just think you need to refit.

That's Gnarly was awesome, but it will divide the ecosystem again, and the ecosystem is becoming one on Warpcast.

Try to use the tools that the ecosystem is using more so it can actually make an impact.

title: NXF Slab Sponsorship Proposal
reason: yo. we have to be equalitarian with budget and proposals, but it's impossible for me to vote against this 100% Gnar surfer. I liked what Brett said in the vote, interesting view. I vote in favor because I know a little about the reality of Bernado and NXF. A group of very psy bodyboarders,  I've never seen anything like it. The point is, they map these waves, and give their lives to build a scene around them for years, and today it has global repercussion and global recognition... however they are having difficulty continuing this movement because they have no sponsorship, jet ski fucked up situation really dangerous in the ocean. I vote in favor of this proposal because the situation is precarious and the movement is fucking true. If the proposal passes, I would love to see a video of you saying that GnarsDAO helped to renovate the jetski and resources enabling the continuation of this dream and construction of this journey.

title: Gnars Sunset IRL Docuseries Premiere Event with Open Sky Cinema
reason:
Aloha, rickyday sunny! 

Don't take it personally, on the contrary, it's just a point of view. :)

I agree with the points that skatehacker.eth, zimardrp.eth and 
luizhadad.eth, they raised important points for our community! 

From my perspective, I acknowledge your desire for Rickyday to assume the role of Sunny's manager. However, I believe it's essential for him to play a more active role in our meetings and developmental processes.

I also advocate for his involvement in voting on various proposals and sharing his projects, ideas, and insights. Given his unique perspective as one of the few Black individuals in our community, his input holds particular value and can enrich our collective discussions.

I really believe that this can inspire so many other people.
